title: CP unveils university partnership to transform education
desc: Charoen Pokphand (CP) Group has partnered with Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool University (XJTLU) to launch a "syntegrative" education centre that can disrupt the industry in Thailand, positioning the nation as a global learning hub.

title: Lawmaker pensions stir big debate
desc: Lawmakers have expressed mixed views over scrapping a pension fund for former MPs and senators, amid concerns over fairness and long-term sustainability.

title: Ex-MP wants concession  deal probe
desc: Former Democrat MP Chanchai Issarasenarak has petitioned the National Anti‑Corruption Commission (NACC) to investigate Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, the cabinet and the board of Airports of Thailand (AoT) over amendments to duty‑free concession contracts that he claims caused up to 180 billion baht in losses to the state.

title: Energy Ministry battens down amid 'worst crisis'
desc: The Energy Ministry's permanent secretary, Prasert Sinsukprasert, has described the current energy situation as one of the most severe crises in Thailand's history.

title: PTG denies fuel profiteering allegations
desc: PTG Energy, a SET-listed oil trader under the PT brand, has dismissed speculation that it was hoarding fuel for profit, adding it has no plans to pursue legal action against those spreading the accusations.

title: Liquidity pressures rise in Thai bond market
desc: The corporate bond market faces a critical test this year, as heightened refinancing risks amid a slowing economy and escalating geopolitical tensions threaten roughly 687 billion baht in bonds set to mature over the remaining three quarters, according to the Thai Bond Market Association (ThaiBMA).

title: Oil refineries in the spotlight
desc: A proposal for the government to intervene in oil refinery costs and profits was not included as one of the seven measures recently unveiled to ease the consumer impact of surging global crude oil prices.

title: Possible security services link to MP’s shooting probed
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has ordered a swift investigation into allegations that a vehicle used in an attack on a southern MP was linked to the Internal Security Operations Command (Isoc), as authorities move to clarify the circumstances surrounding the incident.

title: Korean loan shark gang leader arrested in Pathum Thani
desc: PATHUM THANI - Police have arrested the alleged leader of a South Korean illegal lending network accused of defrauding more than 9,000 victims out of over 355 million baht, after he fled to Thailand to evade prosecution.

title: Kalasin beauty queen loses 2.6m baht to ‘occult’ scam
desc: KHON KAEN: A provincial beauty queen is pursuing legal action after allegedly being duped into spending more than 2.6 million baht on amulets and rituals promised to restore her love life.

title: 57 million litres of fuel ‘missing’ from Surat Thani depots
desc: Irregularities have been found in the disappearance of 57 million litres of fuel during transport to six oil depots in the southern province of Surat Thani, according to Justice Minister Rutthaphon Naowarat.

title: Post Malone returns to Bangkok with 'Big Ass World Tour'
desc: The 9x diamond-certified global superstar (and one of the most-streamed artists globally to boot) Post Malone will return to Asia in September and October with his "Big Ass World Tour", with special guest Don Toliver.

title: Cement truck falls from bridge onto Bangna-Trat highway
desc: SAMUT PRAKAN – A cement mixer truck crashed through the barrier of a U-turn bridge and plunged onto the Bangna-Trat Highway (Theparat Road) on Friday afternoon, blocking two lanes of traffic and leaving its driver critically injured.

title: Guru's Weekly Buzz: April 3-9
desc: Guru By Bangkok Post's weekly pick of the most exciting products, activities, food and travel to indulge in.

title: PM orders DSI to probe suspected oil hoarding
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on Friday revealed evidence of widespread oil hoarding and market manipulation, pledging swift legal action and ordering a special investigation to expand the probe to those involved.

title: MPC member vacates seat amid reports to join finance minister
desc: A member of the Bank of Thailand’s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), Santitarn Sathirathai, has left the seven-member panel amid reports he is set to join a team led by Finance Minister Ekniti Nitithanpraphas.

title: 谁动了泰国5700万升燃油？政府下令彻查

title: 泰总理：别抢油！宋干节不会缺油
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，泰国总理阿努廷就当前国内燃油供应情况作出说明。他表示，政府已提前做好应对准备，确保宋干节假期期间全国燃油供应正常...

title: 宋干节出行注意！廊曼机场提醒：至少提前3小时到机场
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 随着泰国宋干节假期临近，曼谷廊曼国际机场于4月3日发布出行提示，建议计划在节日期间乘机出行的旅客，至少提前2至3小时抵达机场办理手续...

title: 泰国公主诗琳通将访华
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月3日，外交部发言人宣布：应中国政府邀请，泰王国玛哈扎克里·诗琳通公主殿下将于4月4日至11日访问中国。
